title: discovery_tokens and file-context: where the receipts math and PreToolUse(Read) injection live
summary: Per packet discovery cost lives under quality.discovery tokens capture in mcp/kernel.ts; conservative per type defaults in DEFAULT DISCOVERY TOKENS, flagged discovery tokens estimated when not caller reported; auto disti
body:
Per-packet discovery cost lives under quality.discovery_tokens (capture() in mcp/kernel.ts; conservative per-type defaults in DEFAULT_DISCOVERY_TOKENS, flagged discovery_tokens_estimated when not caller-reported; auto-distill estimates from session observation material). Recall receipts take max(read-vs-source, replayTokensSaved()) so savings never regress; replay_tokens flows into the value ledger and the 'Knowledge replay value' line in kage gains. kageFileContext() (kernel.ts) returns <=3 verified packets citing a file — it reuses staleMemoryReasons() so ANY stale reason excludes a packet; CLI command is 'kage file-context --project <dir> --path <file>'. The Claude Code PreToolUse(Read) hook kage-read-context.sh is generated in setupAgent() and vendored in plugin/hooks/kage-read-context.sh (keep both in sync); it dedups one injection per file per session via /tmp/kage-read-context/<sha(session|path)> state files and always exits 0.
Verified by: npm test in mcp/: 220 tests pass, including new tests for discovery_tokens defaults, receipt floor math, and file-context verification gating
